response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with strong testing culture, open-source contributions, and some genuinely relevant high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby, but he misses the role's core minimum qualification by a wide margin: the job requires 8 years of C++, while his resume shows only about seven months of professional C/C++ work at National Instruments in 2014-2015, with his career centered on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang instead. His large-scale infrastructure and cloud architecture experience is also well short of the 5-year bar, and his leadership experience is mentoring-through-workshops rather than owning technical strategy for a compute platform, making him a poor fit for this specific technical lead position despite solid general seniority.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience (microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby)
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, multi-day courses, code dojos, conference talks)
Deep software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, authored testing libraries and open-source infrastructure projects
concerns: Far below the required 8 years of C++ experience (~7 months professional C/C++ work)
Limited large-scale cloud/compute platform experience relative to the 5-year requirement; cloud work is mostly application-level AWS/Kubernetes
No formal technical-leadership role owning cross-team strategy, priorities, and deliverables as this role demands

key_factors.factor: Limited cloud compute platform and infrastructure depth
